title: SACRAMENTO

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "E-bikes can reduce car trips and promote healthy living.",
    "A good quality e-bike can cost upwards of $1,200, making them unaffordable for many Californians.",
    "Subsidizing electric bikes is a more cost-effective strategy for reducing greenhouse gas emissions from passenger cars than electric vehicle incentive programs.",
    "If 15 percent of total commuter miles traveled were by e-bike, a 12 percent reduction in greenhouse gas emissions from transportation could be achieved.",
    "More than fifteen percent of vehicle trips in California are less than one mile.",
    "Eighty percent of vehicle trips in California are less than ten miles.",
    "Nearly half (46 percent) of e-bike trips replace car commutes.",
    "The average e-bike trip is 9.3 miles long.",
    "Increasing the number of trips made by bicycle is essential to reducing vehicle miles traveled and achieving greenhouse gas reduction goals."
  ]
}
```
